{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided, HBsAg is the marker that persists in chronic hepatitis. The documents mention that HBsAg is present in chronic hepatitis B infection and can remain positive for more than 6 months after the initial infection. Recurrent hepatitis can also be associated with chronic hepatitis, so HBsAg would persist in such cases. IgG Anti HbcAg, IgG Anti HBsAG, and Anti Hbs do not persist in chronic hepatitis as they are markers of past infection or immunity.", "answer_choice": "B"}
